prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for furan (9 atoms: O, C, C, C, C, H, H, H, H) using DFT at tier 'standard' (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fit, PySCF RKS), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1 for the neutral; charge +1, multiplicity 2 for the cation (N−1); charge −1, multiplicity 2 for the anion (N+1). Geometry was taken as-is from the user-supplied furan.xyz (no re-optimization at DFT level). Partial charges were computed via Mulliken population analysis (PySCF). All three SCFs converged; f⁺ and f⁻ both sum to 1.0000 (charge conservation passed). Integrity status: ok, trustworthy: true. No warnings reported by the engine.

The headline value reported is the largest f⁺ (electrophilic Fukui index), found at atom 5 (C, α-carbon): f⁺ = 1.9242931. The α-carbons (C4, C5) show the largest positive dual descriptors (~+1.76), marking them as the primary sites for electrophilic aromatic substitution, consistent with the known α-selectivity of furan. The β-carbons (C2, C3) are secondary EAS sites (dual descriptor ~+1.14). Negative Fukui values on O and H atoms are a known artifact of the finite-difference/Mulliken approach.

CLI invocation: cli.py fukui --method dft --tier standard furan.xyz --out live.json
Plot: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/fukui-reactivity-validation/furan/argo_claude-sonnet-4.6/20260702-200658_furan_fukui_reactivity/agent_call_01_plot.png
